title: Global Water Footprint Accounting
summary: Mapping the spatial and temporal patterns of freshwater consumption embedded in goods, crops, and supply chains, and tracing the drivers behind shifting demand.

title: Agricultural Water Productivity
summary: Benchmarking how efficiently crops convert water into yield across regions and management practices, to identify where productivity gains are most achievable.

title: Water Scarcity & Risk Indicators
summary: Examining how the choice of indicator and temporal aggregation method changes our read on where and when water scarcity occurs — and what that means for policy.

title: Food–Energy–Water Nexus
summary: Studying how water, energy, and food systems interact and compete for the same finite resources, with an eye toward sustainable trade-offs.
